title: Render a true colored comic with panel parts (not just dialogue text)

requirements.text: Replace the current string-only story/credits data model with a structured comic panel model that supports distinct comic parts (e.g., panel title/scene label, narration/caption boxes, speech bubbles, thought bubbles, and sound-effect text), so the UI can render an actual comic instead of a transcript.

quotes: It is the actual comic with colors and the comic panels not just pure dialoug it has to have correct comic parts and color scheme's

acceptanceCriteria: Story content is represented as an array of panel objects (not only strings) with explicit fields for at least: panel kind/type (story/credits), and at least one of: caption/narration, dialogue (speaker + text), SFX text.
ComicViewer renders panels from the structured model without TypeScript errors.
Credits are still shown, but rendered using the same structured model rather than a plain string list.

requirements.text: Update the comic reading experience to visually look like a real comic: render panel frames with consistent comic styling, and within each panel render distinct comic parts with clear visual differentiation (caption boxes, speech bubbles, SFX typography) and a coherent, intentional color scheme across panels.

quotes: It is the actual comic with colors and the comic panels not just pure dialoug it has to have correct comic parts and color scheme's

acceptanceCriteria: Each panel visually includes at least two distinct comic elements (e.g., a caption box + a speech bubble, or a speech bubble + SFX) where applicable based on the panel data.

Panels use the existing comic-inspired palette (Tailwind/theme variables) with deliberate color usage for different part types (e.g., captions vs speech vs SFX) while maintaining readability/contrast in both light and dark mode.
The app’s top-level labels no longer describe the content as a script-only viewer (e.g., update header/subtitle text that currently says “Script Viewer” to “Comic Viewer” or equivalent).
All user-facing text remains in English.

requirements.text: Ensure the existing repetition-removal behavior remains compatible with the new structured panel model by deduplicating repeated panel text content in a predictable way (e.g., deduplicate repeated dialogue/caption/SFX lines across panels while preserving the first occurrence).
acceptanceCriteria: When repetition removal is enabled, repeated identical text lines found in panel parts (dialogue/captions/SFX) are removed or suppressed consistently, while the overall panel ordering remains stable.
When repetition removal is disabled, the comic renders all original panel text content.
The existing repetition removal toggle continues to work and the UI does not regress (no crashes, no empty render) with the new panel model.

constraints: Do not edit files under frontend/src/components/ui (read-only UI component sources must be composed, not edited).
Do not edit frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts or frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts or frontend/src/main.tsx (immutable paths).
Keep all user-facing text in English.
nonGoals: Generating or integrating AI-produced comic artwork/images.
Adding PDF generation/export or ReportLab-based rendering in this change (focus on the in-app comic rendering).
Adding new authentication methods beyond Internet Identity.
